column4: Arab Spring protests met with brutal crackdown. Multi-faction civil war drew in Russia, Iran, Turkey, US, ISIS. 500,000+ killed. Assad fell Dec 8, 2024 when HTS-led offensive collapsed the regime. Assad fled to Russia.bushcenter+1
column5: HTS-led transitional authority vs. Kurdish SDF (US-backed), ISIS remnants, various local armed groups, Turkish-backed factions. Israel conducting strikes.
column6: No unified government. HTS attempting governance transition but armed groups compete for territory. Escalating violence in northeast between armed factions. Israel struck targets inside Syria repeatedly. 12+ million displaced. 16+ million need humanitarian assistance. Curfews widespread.bushcenter+2
column7: Syria post-Assad looks disturbingly like Libya post-Gaddafi. HTS is attempting to rebrand from jihadist militia to governing authority, but it lacks legitimacy, resources, and the ability to project power across all of Syria. The Kurds will not submit to HTS rule. Turkey will not allow Kurdish autonomy. ISIS will exploit every vacuum. Russia has lost its primary regional client. The reconstruction cost is hundreds of billions that nobody will pay. Most likely: a years-long period of fragmented authority, periodic clashes, and continued displacement, with Turkey and Israel being the primary external shapers of outcomes.bushcenter+1

column4: Hezbollah formed during Israeli occupation of Lebanon. 2006 war ended in stalemate. Hezbollah opened fire in solidarity with Hamas after Oct 7. Escalated dramatically with the 2026 Iran war.wfpusa
column5: Israel (IDF) vs. Hezbollah (Iranian-backed). Lebanese state is a bystander.
column6: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River (~30km into Lebanon). Hezbollah launching rockets at northern Israel (Kiryat Shmona). IDF issuing evacuation orders for 13 Lebanese villages. Ceasefire from earlier deal has collapsed.wfpusa
column7: Israel is attempting to create a permanent buffer zone in southern Lebanon, which will produce a long-term occupation scenario. Hezbollah is degraded after losing much of its leadership and Iranian supply lines, but it remains capable of guerrilla warfare in its home terrain. Israel learned nothing from its 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon (1982-2000) and is repeating the same strategic error. Crossing the Litani does not eliminate Hezbollah; it creates a new front that bleeds IDF resources indefinitely.wfpusa

column3: Nov 2020 (Tigray war); Pretoria cessation of hostilities Nov 2022
column4: Tigray war killed 600,000+. Pretoria agreement ended major fighting but Amhara-Fano insurgency, Oromia conflict, and Tigray disarmament disputes persist.globalr2p
column5: Ethiopian federal government (ENDF) vs. Fano militia (Amhara), OLA (Oromo Liberation Army), residual Tigrayan tensions. Eritrea complicates Tigray situation.
column6: Low-intensity Amhara insurgency ongoing. Fano controls rural areas in Amhara region. OLA active in western Oromia. Tigray reconstruction stalled. Eritrean troops not fully withdrawn.
column7: Ethiopia avoided state collapse but the underlying ethnic federalism tensions are unresolved. PM Abiy's centralization project is generating armed resistance in Amhara and Oromia simultaneously. The Tigray ceasefire holds but Tigray's economy is devastated and Eritrea remains a spoiler. Ethiopia is managing multiple low-grade insurgencies that could re-escalate if any single front deteriorates.

column4: Post-9/11 dynamics, tribal areas instability, Afghan Taliban spillover. India-Pakistan tensions escalated to direct exchanges of fire in 2025--the worst in decades.csis
column5: Pakistan Armed Forces vs. TTP (Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan), Baloch Liberation Army (BLA), ISIS-K. India-Pakistan tensions overlay.
column6: ACLED reports Pakistan became more dangerous for civilians in past 12 months. Deaths from political violence increased. Regional insurgencies growing. India-Pakistan military escalation (short-lived but intense) occurred in 2025.csis
column7: Pakistan's security situation is deteriorating on multiple fronts simultaneously. The Afghan Taliban's refusal to crack down on TTP sanctuaries in Afghanistan guarantees continued cross-border attacks. Balochistan independence movement is intensifying with increasingly sophisticated attacks. The India-Pakistan nuclear dynamic means any conventional escalation carries existential risk. Pakistan's economic crisis limits its ability to fund counter-insurgency at the required scale.csis

column4: Formerly one of Latin America's safest countries, Ecuador became a transit hub for Colombian cocaine. Prison massacres, gang infiltration of state institutions, and the assassination of presidential candidate Villavicencio (2023) marked the tipping point.
column5: Ecuadorian Armed Forces + Police vs. Los Choneros, Los Lobos, Latin Kings, and transnational cartel affiliates (Sinaloa, CJNG proxies).
column6: 1,000+ more killed from political violence than in 2024. ACLED ranks Ecuador among the top 10 most severe conflicts globally. President Noboa declared internal armed conflict in Jan 2024 and deployed military.csis
column7: Ecuador is the cautionary tale for what happens when cartel economics overwhelm weak institutions. The military crackdown has contained the worst prison violence but gangs have dispersed into rural areas and smaller cities. Ecuador lacks the intelligence infrastructure and judicial capacity to dismantle networks. Without addressing the cocaine transit economy (which requires Colombia and US cooperation), this is a permanent new normal.

column4: Decades of favela-based gang warfare (PCC, Comando Vermelho) combined with expanding militia control in urban peripheries. Drug trafficking, extortion, illegal logging.
column5: Brazilian state/police/military vs. PCC (Primeiro Comando da Capital), Comando Vermelho, urban militias.
column6: ACLED ranks Brazil among the top 10 most severe conflicts globally based on deadliness and civilian danger.csis
column7: Brazil's security crisis is structural and permanent at current policy settings. The country's extreme inequality, weak policing, and massive prison population (which functions as gang recruitment infrastructure) guarantee continued violence. Lula's government has no strategy that differs meaningfully from predecessors.

column4: Ethnic conflict over Nagorno-Karabakh. Azerbaijan won decisively in 2020 and 2023, ethnically cleansing 100,000+ Armenians. Peace deal initialed Aug 8, 2025 at White House with Trump mediation.aljazeera+1
column5: Armenia vs. Azerbaijan. Turkey backs Azerbaijan. Russia (former Armenia patron) distracted by Ukraine. US mediating.
column6: Armenia's intelligence service assesses war in 2026 as "highly unlikely." Peace deal initialed but not ratified. Constitutional issues (Armenia's reference to Karabakh) and transit corridor dispute remain unresolved. Azerbaijan holds all leverage.dialogueinitiatives+1
column7: This conflict is transitioning from war to coerced peace. Azerbaijan won comprehensively and Armenia has no choice but to accept terms. The "Trump Route" transit corridor gives the US a stake in implementation. The peace will hold because Armenia has no military option and Russia cannot protect it. But it is a victor's peace--Armenia loses Karabakh permanently, accepts territorial concessions, and gains nothing except the absence of further war. Azerbaijani irredentism toward southern Armenia (Syunik corridor) remains a latent risk.aljazeera+2

column3: 2011 (NATO intervention / civil war); Second civil war 2014-2020
column4: Gaddafi overthrown 2011. Country split between rival governments (Tripoli-based GNU vs. eastern Tobruk-based HoR/LNA under Haftar). Proxy war involving Turkey, UAE, Egypt, Russia.
column5: Government of National Unity (GNU, Tripoli) vs. Libyan National Army (LNA, Haftar, eastern), various militias, ISIS remnants. Turkey backs GNU; UAE/Egypt/Russia back LNA.
column6: Low-intensity standoff. No elections held despite years of planning. Oil production continues but revenue distribution is contested. Russian Africa Corps presence in east. Occasional militia clashes.
column7: Libya is a frozen conflict with two governments and no path to reunification. Both sides have enough external backing to survive but not enough to conquer the other. Elections will not happen because neither side will accept results it might lose. Libya will remain a split state with periodic violence, functioning primarily as a transit point for migration to Europe and a weapons bazaar for the Sahel.
